dialogue.line: Hey Stewie, you know what an API is?
So like, an API is basically a waiter at a restaurant.
That is a grotesque oversimplification, but continue.
You go to a restaurant, you don't walk into the kitchen and start cooking. You tell the waiter what you want.
So the waiter is the intermediary between you and the kitchen.
Exactly! The API is the waiter. Your app talks to the API, the API talks to the server, gets the data, brings it back.
And the menu would be the API documentation. It tells you what you can order.
See? You get it! REST API, GraphQL, whatever. Same concept. You ask, it delivers.
I suppose even a simpleton can grasp distributed systems with the right analogy.
That's the nicest thing you've ever said to me.
